questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Choice A strengthens the argument because it gives weight to the supporting premise; if the normal rate is .01% then 39% is surely an alarming number. Choice B weakens the argument by contradiction.

choices.a: Many of the world's problems can be solved only by highly specialised experts working on specific problems.
choices.b: Relatively few generalists are needed to coordinate the work of the many specialists.
choices.c: Specialisation does not necessarily entail losing the ability to see the broad picture.
